fault_hypothesis: Lines 74-75 incorrectly pop the last element when both streams share the same last value, removing a valid duplicate that should be kept according to the 'keep_both' strategy.

constraints.intent: The pop() at line 75 should never execute because it incorrectly removes a valid duplicate element.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition triggering the pop should never be true since duplicates at the end must be preserved.
Execution should never enter the then-branch of the condition at line 74 because it incorrectly removes a duplicate.
The merged result must contain exactly as many elements as both input streams combined.
